questions.q: Balban was a Sultan of which Delhi Sultanate dynasty?
questions.explanation: Balban belonged to the Mamluk or Slave dynasty, the first dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.q: In which century did Balban rule as Sultan of Delhi?
questions.explanation: Balban ruled as Sultan in the later 13th century CE, becoming Sultan in 1266.

questions.q: Balban's reign is best remembered for restoring what?
questions.explanation: Balban restored strong royal authority after a period of weakness in the Sultanate.

questions.q: Which city served as the capital of the Sultanate during Balban's reign?
questions.explanation: Balban ruled the Delhi Sultanate from its capital at Delhi.

questions.q: Balban was known for enforcing what at his court?
questions.explanation: Balban enforced strict court etiquette and ceremony to elevate the prestige of the throne.

questions.q: Before becoming Sultan, Balban held which powerful office under earlier rulers?
questions.explanation: Balban served as naib or deputy, effectively running the state before taking the throne himself.

questions.q: Balban dealt with internal revolts and external threats with what kind of approach?
questions.explanation: Balban crushed internal revolts and external threats with a firm and often ruthless hand.

questions.q: Balban rose to prominence as part of an elite group of slave-nobles. What were these nobles known as?
questions.explanation: Balban was originally one of the elite slave-nobles known as 'the Forty' or Chahalgani/Turkan-i-Chihalgani.

questions.q: The Delhi Sultanate over which Balban ruled was a state of what character?
questions.explanation: The Delhi Sultanate was a Muslim state ruling large parts of medieval India.

questions.q: Which threat from the northwest did Balban have to guard the Sultanate against?
questions.explanation: Balban had to defend the Sultanate's northwestern frontier against repeated Mongol incursions.

questions.q: In what year did Balban formally ascend the throne as Sultan of Delhi?
questions.explanation: Balban ascended the throne in 1266 CE after the death of Sultan Nasir-ud-din Mahmud.

questions.q: What royal title did Balban formally adopt, reflecting his exalted view of kingship?
questions.explanation: Balban adopted the title Zill-i-Ilahi, the 'Shadow of God', to emphasise the divine prestige of the monarchy.

questions.q: Balban became Sultan after the death of which preceding ruler, whom he had long served as deputy?
questions.explanation: Balban succeeded Nasir-ud-din Mahmud, under whom he had served as the powerful naib or deputy.

questions.q: Which two Persian court ceremonies did Balban introduce to glorify the throne?
questions.explanation: Balban introduced the Persian customs of Sijda (prostration) and Paibos (kissing the king's feet) before the Sultan.

questions.q: To curb threats to his power, Balban deliberately destroyed the influence of which group of nobles?
questions.explanation: Balban broke the power of 'the Forty', the group of slave-nobles to which he had once belonged, to secure absolute authority.

questions.q: Balban suppressed a major rebellion in which eastern province led by its governor Tughril Khan?
questions.explanation: Balban crushed the revolt of Tughril Khan, the rebellious governor of Bengal, and punished the region harshly.

questions.q: What did Balban name the elder son and chosen heir whose death in battle deeply grieved him?
questions.explanation: Balban's favourite son and heir, Prince Muhammad, was killed fighting the Mongols, a loss that is said to have hastened the Sultan's own death.

questions.q: To strengthen administration, Balban established a strong central institution focused on what function?
questions.explanation: Balban built an efficient network of spies and informers (the barid system) to keep watch over nobles and provinces.

questions.q: Approximately when did Balban's reign end with his death?
questions.explanation: Balban died in 1287 CE after roughly two decades on the throne.

questions.q: Balban policed the troublesome forested region around Delhi inhabited by which raiding community?
questions.explanation: Balban cleared the forests near Delhi and suppressed the Mewatis, robbers who had endangered the capital's surroundings.

questions.q: Balban's general approach to the frontier with the Mongols was largely one of what?
questions.explanation: Rather than distant campaigns, Balban pursued a defensive policy, strengthening forts and the army to hold the frontier against the Mongols.

questions.q: Who immediately succeeded Balban on the throne of Delhi after his death?
questions.explanation: Balban was succeeded by his grandson Kaiqubad, though the dynasty's power soon passed to the Khaljis.

questions.q: What was Balban's personal name before he rose through the ranks as a slave-noble?
questions.explanation: Balban's original name was Baha-ud-din; he later took the regnal name Ghiyas-ud-din Balban as Sultan.

questions.q: Under which earlier Sultan was Balban purchased and brought into service before his rise to power?
questions.explanation: Balban was bought as a slave and entered royal service under Sultan Iltutmish, later becoming one of his elite Forty.

questions.q: Balban claimed grand descent from which legendary line to bolster his royal prestige?
questions.explanation: Balban claimed descent from the legendary Turkish hero Afrasiyab of the Ilbari tribe to legitimise his exalted kingship.

questions.q: Which contemporary Persian-language historian and poet flourished at Balban's court?
questions.explanation: The celebrated poet Amir Khusrau began his literary career under the patronage of Balban and his son Prince Muhammad.

questions.q: Balban built a strong line of defence on the northwestern frontier by garrisoning and fortifying which key region?
options: The area around Lahore and the Sutlej
questions.explanation: Balban refortified the northwestern frontier around Lahore and the Sutlej line to block Mongol incursions.

questions.q: Balban's political philosophy of kingship is often summarised by his insistence that the Sultan stood far above whom?
questions.explanation: Balban held that the Sultan must remain aloof and far above the ordinary nobility to preserve the awe and majesty of kingship.

questions.q: Which dynasty seized power within a few years of Balban's death, ending Mamluk rule in Delhi?
questions.explanation: Within a few years of Balban's death, Jalaluddin Khalji founded the Khalji dynasty, supplanting the Mamluks.

questions.q: Balban's reputation as the 'Iron Sultan' chiefly rests on his policy of what, sometimes called 'blood and iron'?
questions.explanation: Balban is remembered for a stern 'blood and iron' policy, ruthlessly suppressing rebels and rivals to enforce order.
